_readme: What the tap-to-hear button SPEAKS, when the bare 漢字 would be read with the wrong reading.
Speech synthesis reads the CHARACTER and picks the reading itself — our zhuyin/pinyin never
reaches the voice. Bound particles are the problem: 的/了/著 never occur alone in speech, so a
voice handed one in isolation falls back to its standalone reading and 著 comes out zhù.
Keep these to the MINIMUM, on both counts:
- only add one where the bare character is genuinely misread — you are trading away 'I tapped
a word and heard that word', so an unnecessary override makes the audio worse, not better
- use the SHORTEST context that forces the reading (two characters is almost always enough).
Do not use a full sentence: the button should still sound like the word.
NOT fixable this way: 和 (and). A Taiwan voice reads it hàn, which is a real Taiwanese reading
of the conjunction — no context forces hé, because hàn is the reading the voice knows.

18.why: 的 is bound; alone it can be read dì. 我的 forces de.

19.why: 了 is bound; alone it is read liǎo. 好了 forces le.

21.why: 著 is bound; alone it was reported as zhù. 開著 forces zhe.
